topic: How to Run AI Models Locally: Ollama vs LM Studio in 2026

key_points: Ollama is infrastructure, LM Studio is an application
Both use llama.cpp under the hood, near-identical raw speed
LM Studio faster on Apple Silicon via MLX (237 vs 149 tok/s for Gemma 3 1B on M3 Ultra)
Many users use both: LM Studio for exploration, Ollama for production
Ollama 52M monthly downloads in Q1 2026 (520x growth from 2023)
Models deliver 70-85% of frontier quality at zero marginal cost
50K daily requests: cloud $2,250/mo vs local electricity only
LM Studio best HuggingFace integration for model discovery
Jan adds cloud API integration alongside local inference
LocalAI as universal API hub for multiple backends
Llama 3.3 8B best for 8GB RAM (4.9GB on disk)
Phi-4 14B outperforms 30-70B models on structured tasks with 16GB
Qwen 2.5 Coder 14B top local coding model

stats.fact: Ollama hit 52 million monthly downloads in Q1 2026, up from 100K in Q1 2023 (520x growth)
HuggingFace hosts 135,000 GGUF-formatted models for local inference, up from 200 three years ago
Enterprise local deployment: 25% strictly local, 30% hybrid, 45% cloud API
Local models deliver 70-85% of frontier model quality at zero marginal cost
RTX 4090: 145 tokens/second for 8B models, limited to 32B by 24GB VRAM
M4 Max (128GB): runs 70B DeepSeek-R1 at 12 tok/s
LM Studio 237 tok/s vs Ollama 149 tok/s on M3 Ultra (Gemma 3 1B) due to MLX backend
GGUF quantization compresses models to 25-30% of original size with minimal quality loss
50K daily requests: cloud APIs ~$2,250/mo vs local setup marginal cost only electricity (<$15/mo)
Mac Studio M4 Max amortized: $139/mo over 36 months; custom RTX 4090 PC: $55/mo
Phi-4-mini (3.8B) runs on 8GB RAM consuming ~3.5GB at Q4_K_M, delivers 15-20 tok/s on M1 MacBook Air

ollama.best_for: Developers, production deployments, Docker, API integration
cons: Slower on Apple Silicon vs LM Studio MLX

lm_studio.best_for: Model exploration, interactive chat, Apple Silicon users, beginners
lm_studio.pricing: Free for personal and commercial use

others.name: Jan
others.niche: ChatGPT-style desktop assistant with cloud API fallback
others.unique: Docker image for headless server, IDE integrations

others.name: GPT4All
others.unique: LocalDocs RAG built in — no other tool has this natively

others.name: LocalAI
others.niche: Universal API hub for multiple backends
others.unique: Routes to multiple inference backends, MCP integration, distributed inference
